title: Connected Perplexity Computer to Public's trading API- full setup walkthrough
author: BuildwithPublic
selftext:
Been working on something at Public that I think this sub would find interesting.

Most API integrations with AI tools stop at the same place — you get data, maybe a dashboard, maybe some trade ideas. Then you alt-tab to your broker and manually enter the order. The AI part ends where the actual trading begins.

The Public API is different. It's not just a data feed. It has a full execution layer — order preflight, multileg options support, the whole thing. So when you plug it into something like Perplexity Computer, you get a workflow where you go from "show me the SPY options chain" to "preflight this iron condor" to reviewing a structured order — all in one conversation.

I recorded a 10-minute walkthrough showing the full setup:

* Install the Public agent skill in Perplexity Computer
* Connect API credentials through natural language (no config files)
* Pull live quotes and account data
* Analyze an options chain with straddle pricing and skew
* Preflight a multileg order with defined risk

This isn't a chatbot that gives you trade ideas and wishes you luck. It's an actual brokerage workflow running through an AI agent.

Happy to answer questions about the integration or the API itself.

https://reddit.com/link/1sloaky/video/hp78wd5ch8vg1/player

comments.author: AssignmentDull5197
comments.body: This is the part most agent demos skip, the last mile from analysis to execution. The preflight / structured order review in the same convo is a really nice safety step too. How are you handling guardrails so the agent cannot place orders without an explicit confirmation step, and do you log the reasoning/context for audit? If you are writing up best practices, this has a good overview of chatbot/agent safety patterns and evaluation that maps well to trading style workflows: https://www.chatbase.co/blog

comments.author: Careless-Diamond-277
comments.body: Possible to See and Test mysel
